reason: Map the React, Tauri setup, and Python sidecar engine/model flow without contract drift.
Reuse the existing engine registry, setup profiles, model jobs, and dual sidecar tree intentionally.
Apply the ASR sidecar ownership boundary and pre-development checklist.
Implement and register the optional ReazonSpeech engine with honest availability and model cache behavior.
Preserve model download and transcription job contracts, progress, cancellation, and camelCase snapshots.
Add focused unittest coverage and report optional real-model validation accurately.
Keep UI/settings behavior in React and use existing typed services and components.
Use existing controls, Simplified Chinese copy, and avoid invalid ReazonSpeech VAD UI.
Keep ASR setup profile strings aligned across TypeScript and Rust without local casts.
Follow pnpm test/build and minimal-diff requirements for the user-visible workflow.
Apply Tauri ownership and setup/runtime dependency conventions.
Keep optional dependencies and model weights in managed install/cache paths with existing source policy.
Preserve the Tauri-to-sidecar lifecycle and keep inference in Python.
Validate profile wiring, structured process args, and full Rust tests.
Use verified upstream model IDs, NeMo compatibility, timestamp API, licensing, and native long-form tradeoffs.
